title: Frankenstein
subtitle: or, The Modern Prometheus
author: Mary Shelley
note: First read on a dare, kept for the storm scene on the Orkneys.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
condition: Fair — rebound spine, marginalia in pencil
note: Previous owner's notes in the margins are better than most footnotes.

title: Moby-Dick
subtitle: or, The Whale
author: Herman Melville
condition: Worn — water stain on lower corner
note: Bought at a dockside stall; smells faintly of salt to this day.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
condition: Good — ex-library stamp on title page
note: Still has the due-date card pocket glued inside the back cover.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
note: The only book on this shelf I'm afraid to lend out.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
condition: Fair — loose signature at chapter twelve
note: A serialised original in spirit, if not quite in printing.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
note: Took a full winter to read; worth every candle.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Brontë
condition: Worn — moorland damp softened the boards
note: Reads like the wind sounds against the study window.

title: Notre-Dame de Paris
subtitle: The Hunchback of Notre-Dame
author: Victor Hugo
note: Bought the week I first saw the cathedral in person.

title: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
condition: Fair — cover detached, kept in a slipcase
note: Short enough to read by a single candle, end to end.
